Class SwingToolContext

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.ToolContext
uno.anahata.asi.swing.toolkit.SwingToolContext
Direct Known Subclasses:
SwingAgiTool
public class SwingToolContext extends ToolContext
An advanced execution context that bridges the gap between background tool execution and the Swing Event Dispatch Thread (EDT).

This class provides the critical infrastructure for Context Propagation. Since the AI toolchain relies on ThreadLocal state for logging and attachments, moving execution to the EDT would normally break these links. SwingToolContext solves this by capturing the state and re-applying it within the EDT task scope.

Author:
anahata

    Gets a map for sharing objects across all sessions within the current ASI container.

    Gets a shared map for storing state across turns within the current agi session.

    Gets a thread-safe logging consumer that automatically propagates the current tool execution context whenever a message is logged.

    Convenience method to get the metadata for the tool currently being executed.

    peekResponse()
    Internal helper to retrieve the response if it exists, without throwing an exception.
    void
    runAsync(String taskName, Runnable task)
    Executes a task asynchronously in the session's executor service, automatically propagating the current tool execution context to the background thread.

  • Constructor Details

    • SwingToolContext

      public SwingToolContext()

  • Method Details

    • getAgiPanel

      public AgiPanel getAgiPanel()
      Convenience method to get the AgiPanel for this Agi from the java tool.
      Returns:
      the AgiPanel for the current Agi.

    • runInEdt

      public void runInEdt(Runnable runnable)
      Executes a task on the EDT in a non-blocking manner while maintaining full context awareness.

      This is the preferred way for tools to perform UI updates without freezing the AI execution thread.

      Parameters:
      runnable - The UI-bound task to execute.

    • runInEdtAndWait

      public void runInEdtAndWait(Runnable runnable) throws InterruptedException, InvocationTargetException
      Executes a task on the EDT and blocks the current thread until the task is complete, maintaining full context awareness throughout.

      Use this method when subsequent tool logic depends on the UI having reached a specific state (e.g., waiting for a dialog to close or a component to render).

      Parameters:
      runnable - The UI-bound task to execute.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the background thread is interrupted during wait.
      InvocationTargetException - if the EDT task throws an unhandled exception.
